fresh_facts.claim: The U.S. launched Operation Epic Fury against Iran on March 1, 2026, with major combat operations beginning early Saturday.
U.S. forces struck more than 1,000 targets in the first 24 hours of Operation Epic Fury.
More than 100 aircraft launched in a synchronized wave from land bases and carrier decks as part of Operation Epic Fury, including fighters, refueling tankers, bombers, and unmanned systems.
Two carrier strike groups—USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln—are operating in the region during Operation Epic Fury.
Four Americans have been killed since Operation Epic Fury began.
The Pentagon confirmed the loss of three U.S. Air Force F-15E aircraft overnight, though these were not downed by hostile fire.
An Iranian missile penetrated layered air defenses and struck a fortified tactical operations center.
U.S. and partner air defenses have intercepted hundreds of ballistic missiles and one-way attack drones since the Operation Epic Fury campaign began.
Iran struck near the U.S. Navy's Fifth Fleet headquarters in Bahrain during Operation Epic Fury.
Defense Secretary Hegseth declined to outline limits on future action in the conflict with Iran, leaving open the possibility of ground operations.
The Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing, which operates F-35 stealth fighter jets, has deployed as part of ongoing operations against Iran.
The U.S. Navy is preparing to deploy a third aircraft carrier, USS George H.W. Bush, to the Eastern Mediterranean.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died following an Israeli strike on March 5, 2026.
Iran's Assembly of Experts voted to select Mojtaba Khamenei as the next supreme leader around March 8, 2026.
President Trump stated on March 5 that he wants input on Khamenei's successor and deems Mojtaba Khamenei unacceptable.
Strikes on Tehran's fuel infrastructure on March 8 caused black smoke, four deaths, toxic air risks, and fuel disruptions.
Saudi Arabia warned Iran against attacks on its territory or energy sites around March 8, potentially opening bases to U.S. operations.
Gulf states are pursuing diplomacy to prevent wider war, emphasizing regional stability and economic growth, as of March 10.
The US announced escalation of its war on Iran on March 10.
President Pezeshkian shifted from conciliatory to hardline rhetoric, promising decisive responses to military operations.
Ali Safavi of the National Council of Resistance of Iran stated that the Iranian regime is on its last legs regardless of successor.
NCRI/MEK supporters rallied in London on March 6 and in Gothenburg on March 5 for a provisional government post-Khamenei.
Iran's economy is collapsing, exacerbating vulnerability with ecological crises and weakened allies, according to Brookings analysis.
Protests have likely caused high death tolls amid crackdowns starting in December 2025, according to Brookings analysis.
The IDF reported on March 10 that Iranian barrages used few missiles with an implied 50% interception rate.
Mojtaba Khamenei has strengthened ties with the IRGC, with these connections becoming more prominent amid unrest and strikes.

regime_fall.current_status: Operation Epic Fury has delivered sustained intensive strikes across Iranian military infrastructure since March 1, with over 1,000 targets hit in the first 24 hours and three carrier strike groups maintaining operational pressure, while the regime faces critical internal divisions following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death on March 5 and the contested succession of his son Mojtaba amid explicit U.S. rejection of his legitimacy and deepening economic collapse.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei died following an Israeli strike on March 5, 2026, triggering succession crisis with Assembly of Experts selecting Mojtaba Khamenei as successor around March 8 while President Trump explicitly rejected his legitimacy and U.S. forces escalated strikes on fuel infrastructure and airbases.
